lg.l: Before [her], [she] having embraced him, she returned [to the shores]
Like a golden, pretty creeper! [Another lady], having watched
The arriving ships [from] a flower-seashore grove abundant with sand,
Having endured [separation for a long time], [her] husband [finally] arriving,
She relinquished her stone form. [There was] a lady with spear-shaped eyes
Who, as the child of the rival wife [from] the companion group fell,
Having also thrown down her own child into the well, lifted [them both out].
[Then was] a flower-doll[-like] girl [with] hips [like] ripe sapphires,
Who gave up [her] monkey-face at the arrival of [her] husband who had left,
Saying [earlier] to [her] bright face [like] the full moon,
[Upon] noticing the long [= lascivious] glances of another man,
‘May I become [one with] the face of a monkey!’ [In another story,]
As the mother [of a girl] narrated to the father, ‘I did not think,
Having not paid attention to the view of those with fine knowledge,
Who said, ‘The lowly knowledge of women is but foolishness’.
Amidst engaging in play, the vow/reply that I narrated, saying,
‘If I have a girl, and you, o lady [with] bright bracelets have a son,
[He is] the husband that is taken, for her,’ by the excellence of
[Her] overhearing, [her] pain of mind would increase. [Yet],
[She was] a doll[-like] girl [of] golden flowers, who bore [this burden],
Having bowed for a long time to [him as her] leader, having worn Kaliṅga cloth,
Having plaited [her] tresses [in preparation for marriage],
As one who overheard [this conversation], saying, ‘[I] accept [him]
With fortune’. I was born in the city in which girls with tresses
Full of honey who were eternal - girls like her!’ Having thus been affected,
I too am but a wife! If we [i.e., the chaste wives above] are like this,
I cannot abide! I will destroy Maturai along with [its] king.
You will witness my vengeance! Listen, oh the ladies of Kūṭal
With the four high buildings, husbands/men, the Gods in the heavens
And great ascetics! I was enraged the king’s palace that made
A mistake about my beloved man whom I desired. I am not at fault!’
Saying [thus], having wrung with [her] hand [her] left breast,
Having whirled around Maturai rightwards, [saying this] just three times,
Having swung the jewel-breast upon a street full of toddy,
The lady with shining jewels let [it] go. She threw [it].
A celestian man [with] burning arms, in the guise of a Brahmin [with]
Twisted, ruddy, overflowing matter hair of a blue hue,
With white teeth like milk, having appeared in the evening, saying,
‘Oh, great wife! [On] the day that you were wronged [in your] glory,
I am one who possesses an ancient command to feed this city
[With] spreading flames. Who will escape from here?’
By the command of [her with] gold bracelets, having let go of
These people, namely the Brahmins, virtuous people, cows,
Chaste wives, the elderly and the young ones, saying,
‘Join the side of him with the power of fire!’, the smoky heat
That burned crowded over the city of Kūṭal of the ignorant one.
